function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"content": "     1|# dyor-onchain-analytics
     2|
     3|**Category:** trading / onchain-analytics
     4|**Do Yo Own Research — Onchain Analytics Skill**
     5|
     6|Real-time and historical onchain analytics for crypto trading: whale tracking, wallet profiling via DeBank/Footprint, DEX trade flow, smart money detection, and token holder analytics.
     7|
     8|---
     9|
    10|## Capabilities
    11|
    12|### Whale Tracking
    13|- Monitor large transactions (configurable threshold in USD)
    14|- Track sudden token accumulation/distribution patterns
    15|- Alert on whale transfers between exchanges and wallets
    16|- Historical whale activity replay for pattern analysis
    17|
    18|### DeBank Integration
    19|- Portfolio tracking for any ENS or 0x address
    20|- DeBank Pro API for professional data access
    21|- Asset allocation, DeFi positions, transaction history
    22|- Gas spending analytics
    23|
    24|### Footprint Analytics Integration
    25|- NFT floor prices and collection analytics
    26|- DEX liquidity data (Gmx, dYdX, etc.)
    27|- Wallet labeling and cluster analysis
    28|- Cross-chain data (Ethereum, BSC, Polygon, Arbitrum, etc.)
    29|
    30|### DEX Data & Trade Flow
    31|- Real-time trade monitoring on Uniswap, SushiSwap, Curve
    32|- Buy/sell pressure indicators
    33|- DEX liquidity changes and pool creation alerts
    34|- Sandwich attack detection heuristics
    35|
    36|### Smart Money Detection
    37|- Identify known smart money wallets (e.g.,abit of Dragonberries, etc.)
    38|- Track institutional-grade wallets
    39|- Copy-trading signal generation
    40|- Historical P&L of tracked wallets
    41|
    42|---
    43|
    44|## API Keys Required
    45|
    46|```env
    47|DEBANK_API_KEY=*** Free tier: 1000 calls/day
    48|FOOTPRINT_API_KEY=*** Free tier available
    49|ETHERSCAN_API_KEY=*** Free, multiple keys OK
    50|ALCHEMY_API_KEY=*** Free tier, optional
    51|```
    52|
    53|---
    54|
    55|## Usage Examples
    56|
    57|```
    58|@skill dyor-onchain-analytics
    59|
    60|> Track whale activity for 0x...abc (any Ethereum address)
    61|> Show DeBank portfolio for vitalik.eth
    62|> What are the top smart money wallets holding $PEPE?
    63|> Monitor Uniswap v3 USDC/ETH pool for large trades
    64|> Get token holder count and concentration for 0x...xyz
    65|> Alert me when a whale buys >$500k of $XYZ
    66|```
    67|
    68|---
    69|
    70|## Pitfalls
    71|
    72|- **Rate limits**: DeBank free tier is 1000 req/day — cache aggressively
    73|- **Address labels**: DeBank labels are community-sourced, verify independently
    74|- **Whale threshold**: Adjust per token market cap (set too low = noise, too high = missed signals)
    75|- **DEX data lag**: Uniswap subgraphs have ~15min delay on historical queries
    76|- **Wallet clustering**: Heuristic-based; not always accurate for layered privacy
    77|- **Cross-chain**: Footprint covers multiple chains but depth varies significantly
    78|- **Smart money**: Past performance ≠ future results; wallets can exit and reposition
    79|
    80|---
    81|
    82|## Files
    83|
    84|```
    85|dyor-onchain-analytics/
    86|├── skill.md
    87|├── src/
    88|│   ├── __init__.py
    89|│   ├── whale_tracker.py
    90|│   ├── debank_client.py
    91|│   ├── footprint_client.py
    92|│   └── dex_data.py
    93|└── requirements.txt
    94|```
    95|
    96|---
    97|
    98|## Architecture
    99|
   100|```
   101|onchain_analytics/
   102|├── WhaleTracker       # Ethereum/wallet-level tx monitoring
   103|├── DeBankClient       # Portfolio & DeFi position queries
   104|├── FootprintClient    # NFT, cross-chain, labeling data
   105|└── DexDataProvider    # DEX trade flow & liquidity
   106|```
   107|

    50|    def __init__(

    52|        ethereum_rpc: Optional[str] = None,
    53|        etherscan_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    54|        alchemy_api_key: Optional[str] = None,

    68|    # ─── Address tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────────

    80|    # ─── Whale detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

    82|    def get_whale_alerts(
    83|        self,
    84|        token_address: Optional[str] = None,
    85|        min_usd: float = DEFAULT_WHALE_THRESHOLD_USD,
    86|        hours_back: int = 24,
    87|        limit: int = 100,
    88|    ) -> List[WhaleAlert]:
    89|        """
    90|        Fetch recent large transactions involving a token.
    91|        
    92|        Args:
    93|            token_address: Optional ERC20 token address. If None, only ETH txs.
    94|            min_usd: Minimum USD value to flag as whale.
    95|            hours_back: How far back to look.
    96|            limit: Max number of results (Etherscan caps at 10000 but we default 100).

   111|        self._set_cache(cache_key, alerts)

   113|
   114|    def _fetch_via_etherscan(
   115|        self,
   116|        token_address: Optional[str],
   117|        min_usd: float,
   118|        hours_back: int,
   119|        limit: int,
   120|    ) -> List[WhaleAlert]:
   121|        """Use Etherscan API for token transfer events."""
   122|        alerts: List[WhaleAlert] = []
   123|
   124|        if token_address:
   125|            # ERC20 token transfers
   126|            endpoint = "https://api.etherscan.io/api"
   127|            params = {
   128|                "module": "tokentx",
   129|                "action": "tokentx",
   130|                "contractaddress": token_address,
   131|                "sort": "desc",
   132|                "page": 1,
   133|                "offset": limit,
   134|                "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
   135|            }

   140|                "module": "account",
   141|                "action": "txlist",
   142|                "address": "0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000",
   143|                "sort": "desc",
   144|                "page": 1,
   145|                "offset": limit,
   146|                "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
   147|            }
   148|
   149|        try:
   150|            resp = requests.get(endpoint, params=params, timeout=15)
   151|            data = resp.json()
   152|
   153|            if data.get("status") != "1" and data.get("message") != "No transactions found":
   154|                logger.error(f"Etherscan error: {data}")
   155|                return alerts
   156|
   157|            results = data.get("result", [])
   158|            if isinstance(results, str):
   159|                logger.warning(f"Etherscan rate limited or error: {results}")
   160|                return alerts
   161|
   162|            for tx in results:
   163|                value_eth = self._parse_eth_value(tx.get("value", "0"))

   165|
   166|                if value_usd < min_usd:
   167|                    continue
   168|
   169|                alerts.append(
   170|                    WhaleAlert(
   171|                        tx_hash=tx.get("hash", ""),
   172|                        block_number=int(tx.get("blockNumber", 0)),
   173|                        from_address=tx.get("from", ""),
   174|                        to_address=tx.get("to", ""),
   175|                        value_eth=value_eth,
   176|                        value_usd=value_usd,
   177|                        timestamp=int(tx.get("timeStamp", 0)),
   178|                        token_symbol=tx.get("tokenSymbol"),

   180|                        is_suspicious=self._detect_suspicious(tx),
   181|                        notes=self._get_tx_notes(tx),
   182|                    )
   183|                )
   184|        except requests.RequestException as e:
   185|            logger.error(f"Failed to fetch from Etherscan: {e}")
   186|
   187|        return alerts
   188|
   189|    def _fetch_via_rpc(
   190|        self,

   192|        min_usd: float,
   193|        hours_back: int,
   194|        limit: int,
   195|    ) -> List[WhaleAlert]:

   197|        # This is a simplified fallback — full token tracking requires an indexer

   227|    # ─── Address profiling ───────────────────────────────────────────────

   229|    def get_address_summary(self, address: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:

   232|        Uses Etherscan for basic stats.
   233|        """
   234|        cache_key = f"addr_summary:{address}"

   315|    # ─── Exchange flow detection ────────────────────────────────────────
   316|
   317|    def detect_exchange_flow(self, address: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   318|        """
   319|        Heuristic: classify an address as exchange / wallet / contract.
   320|        Uses known exchange address prefixes — not foolproof.

   322|        exchange_prefixes = {

   324|            "0x21a31ee1afc51d94c2efccaa2092ad1028285549": "Binance 9",
   325|            "0x56eddb7aa87536c09ccc2793473599fd21a8b17f": "Binance 10",
   326|            "0x9696f8e268d1a3f6f17bb0a0e6a6a3b2c9e1d5b": "Coinbase 1",
   327|            "0xfbb1b73c4f0bda4f67dca266ce6ef42f520f9e35": "Kraken",
   328|        }
   329|
   330|        addr_lower = address.lower()
   331|        if addr_lower in exchange_prefixes:
   332|            return {"classification": "exchange", "source": "known_list", "name": exchange_prefixes[addr_lower]}
   333|
   334|        summary = self.get_address_summary(address)
   335|        if summary.get("is_contract"):
   336|            return {"classification": "contract", "source": "is_contract", "name": ""}
   337|        if summary.get("tx_count", 0) > 10_000:

   352|    def _get_block_from_timestamp(self, timestamp: int) -> str:
   353|        """Approximate block number from timestamp using ~12s block time."""
   354|        latest_block = 21_000_000  # approximate
   355|        return hex(latest_block)
   356|
   357|    def _detect_suspicious(self, tx: Dict) -> bool:
   358|        """Heuristic for suspicious transaction patterns."""
   359|        input_data = tx.get("input", "0x")
   360|        # Non-standard input data often indicates a contract call
   361|        if input_data != "0x" and input_data != "0x":
   362|            return True
   363|        return False
   364|
   365|    def _get_tx_notes(self, tx: Dict) -> str:
   366|        notes = []
   367|        input_data = tx.get("input", "0x")
   368|        if input_data and input_data != "0x":

   374|        return ", ".join(notes) if notes else "standard_transfer"

   381|            return (time.time() - ts) < self.cache_ttl

{"content": "     1|"""
     2|DeBank Client — portfolio tracking, DeFi positions, and wallet analytics.
     3|Docs: https://open.debank.com/
     4|"""
     5|
     6|import os
     7|import time
     8|import logging
     9|from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any
    10|
    11|import requests
    12|
    13|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    14|
    15|DEBANK_API_KEY=os.get...EY", "")
    16|DEBANK_BASE_URL = "https://api.debank.com"
    17|
    18|
    19|class DeBankClient:
    20|    """
    21|    Query DeBank for wallet portfolios and DeFi positions.
    22|    
    23|    Usage:
    24|        client = DeBankClient()
    25|        portfolio = client.get_portfolio("vitalik.eth")
    26|        positions = client.get_defi_positions("0x...")
    27|        gas = client.get_gas_tracker()
    28|    """
    29|
    30|    def __init__(self, api_key: Optional[str] = None, cache_ttl: int = 120):
    31|        self.api_key = api_key or DEBANK_API_KEY
    32|        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
    33|        self._cache: Dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] = {}
    34|
    35|    # ─── Core endpoints ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    36|
    37|    def get_portfolio(self, address_or_ens: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    38|        """
    39|        Fetch full portfolio for an address or ENS name.
    40|        
    41|        Returns normalized dict with:
    42|          - total_net_usd: float
    43|          - assets: list of token holdings
    44|          - debt_total_usd: float
    45|          - position_list: DeFi positions
    46|        """
    47|        cache_key = f"debank_portfolio:{address_or_ens}"
    48|        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
    49|            return self._get_cached(cache_key)
    50|
    51|        # Resolve ENS if needed
    52|        address = self._resolve_ens(address_or_ens)
    53|
    54|        headers = self._headers()
    55|        if not headers:
    56|            logger.error("DeBank API key required")
    57|            return {}
    58|
    59|        try:
    60|            resp = requests.get(
    61|                f"{DEBANK_BASE_URL}/portfolio",
    62|                params={"address": address},
    63|                headers=headers,
    64|                timeout=15,
    65|            )
    66|            if resp.status_code == 403:
    67|                logger.error("DeBank API key invalid or expired")
    68|                return {}
    69|            resp.raise_for_status()
    70|            raw = resp.json()
    71|
    72|            portfolio = self._normalize_portfolio(raw)
    73|            self._set_cache(cache_key, portfolio)
    74|            return portfolio
    75|
    76|        except requests.RequestException as e:
    77|            logger.error(f"DeBank portfolio fetch failed: {e}")
    78|            return {}
    79|
    80|    def get_defi_positions(self, address: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    81|        """
    82|        Get DeFi protocol positions (lending, LP, staking, etc.).
    83|        
    84|        Returns list of positions, each with:
    85|          - protocol_name, protocol_logo
    86|          - position_type (lend/borrow/lp/stake)
    87|          - supplied_usd, borrowed_usd
    88|          - health_rate
    89|        """

        market = client.get_market_data("ethereum")
        tok = client.get_tokenomics("uniswap")
    """

    def __init__(self, api_key: Optional[str] = None, cache_ttl: int = 60):
        self.api_key = api_key or COINGECKO_API_KEY
        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
        self._cache: Dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] = {}

    # ─── Price ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_price(
        self,
        coin_ids: List[str],
        vs_currencies: List[str] = ["usd"],
        include_24hr_change: bool = True,
    ) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, float]]:
        """
        Get current price(s) for one or more coins.

        Args:
            coin_ids: CoinGecko coin IDs (e.g. ["bitcoin", "ethereum"])
            vs_currencies: Quote currencies (e.g. ["usd", "eth"])
            include_24hr_change: Include 24h % change

        Returns:
            {coin_id: {currency: price, "usd_24h_change": ...}}
        """
        cache_key = f"price:{','.join(coin_ids)}:{','.join(vs_currencies)}:{include_24hr_change}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            params: Dict[str, Any] = {
                "ids": ",".join(coin_ids),
                "vs_currencies": ",".join(vs_currencies),
            }
            if include_24hr_change:
                params["include_24hr_change"] = "true"

            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/simple/price",
                params=params,
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data: Dict[str, Dict[str, float]] = resp.json()
            self._set_cache(cache_key, data)
            return data

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko price fetch failed: {e}")
            return {}

    def get_historical_price(
        self,
        coin_id: str,
        date: str,  # DD-MM-YYYY
        vs_currency: str = "usd",
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get price on a specific date (not real-time).
        """
        cache_key = f"hist_price:{coin_id}:{date}:{vs_currency}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/coins/{coin_id}/history",
                params={"date": date, "location": "world"},
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            result = {
                "coin_id": coin_id,
                "date": date,
                "price": raw.get("market_data", {}).get("current_price", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "market_cap": raw.get("market_data", {}).get("market_cap", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "total_volume": raw.get("market_data", {}).get("total_volume", {}).get(vs_currency),
            }
            self._set_cache(cache_key, result)
            return result

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko historical price failed: {e}")
            return {}

    # ─── Market data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_market_data(
        self,
        coin_id: str,
        vs_currency: str = "usd",
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get full market data for a coin: market cap, volume, supply, ath, atl, etc.
        """
        cache_key = f"market:{coin_id}:{vs_currency}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/coins/{coin_id}",
                params={
                    "localization": "false",
                    "tickers": "false",
                    "market_data": "true",
                    "community_data": "false",
                    "developer_data": "false",
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            md = raw.get("market_data", {})

            result = {
                "coin_id": coin_id,
                "symbol": raw.get("symbol", "").upper(),
                "name": raw.get("name", ""),
                "current_price": md.get("current_price", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "market_cap": md.get("market_cap", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "market_cap_rank": raw.get("market_cap_rank"),
                "total_volume": md.get("total_volume", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "high_24h": md.get("high_24h", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "low_24h": md.get("low_24h", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "price_change_24h": md.get("price_change_24h"),
                "price_change_percentage_24h": md.get("price_change_percentage_24h"),
                "price_change_percentage_7d": md.get("price_change_percentage_7d"),
                "price_change_percentage_30d": md.get("price_change_percentage_30d"),
                "ath": md.get("ath", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "ath_date": md.get("ath_date", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "atl": md.get("atl", {}).get(vs_currency),
                "atl_date": md.get("atl_date", {}).get(vs_currency),
                # Supply
                "circulating_supply": md.get("circulating_supply"),
                "total_supply": md.get("total_supply"),
                "max_supply": md.get("max_supply"),
                # Tokenomics
                "fdv": md.get("fully_diluted_valuation", {}).get(vs_currency),
                # Community
                "social_summary": raw.get("community_data", {}),
            }
            self._set_cache(cache_key, result)
            return result

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko market data failed: {e}")
            return {}

    def get_market_chart(
        self,
        coin_id: str,
        vs_currency: str = "usd",
        days: int = 7,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get price/volume/market cap chart data.
        
        Returns:
            {"prices": [[timestamp, price], ...], "market_caps": [...], "total_volumes": [...]}
        """
        cache_key = f"chart:{coin_id}:{vs_currency}:{days}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/coins/{coin_id}/market_chart",
                params={"vs_currency": vs_currency, "days": days},
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data = resp.json()
            self._set_cache(cache_key, data)
            return data

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko market chart failed: {e}")
            return {}

    def search_coins(self, query: str, limit: int = 10) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Search for coins by name or symbol.
        """
        cache_key = f"search:{query}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/search",
                params={"query": query},
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            coins = raw.get("coins", [])[:limit]
            results = [
                {
                    "id": c.get("id"),
                    "name": c.get("name"),
                    "symbol": c.get("symbol", "").upper(),
                    "market_cap_rank": c.get("market_cap_rank"),
                    "thumb": c.get("thumb"),
                }
                for c in coins
            ]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, results)
            return results

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko search failed: {e}")
            return []

    def get_trending(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get trending coins (CoinGecko trending page).
        """
        cache_key = "trending"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/search/trending",
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            coins = raw.get("coins", [])
            results = [
                {
                    "id": c.get("item", {}).get("id"),
                    "name": c.get("item", {}).get("name"),
                    "symbol": c.get("item", {}).get("symbol", "").upper(),
                    "market_cap_rank": c.get("item", {}).get("market_cap_rank"),
                    "price_btc": c.get("item", {}).get("price_btc"),
                }
                for c in coins
            ]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, results)
            return results

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko trending failed: {e}")
            return []

    # ─── On-chain / tokenomics ────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_tokenomics(self, coin_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Extract tokenomics data for a coin.
        
        Returns:
            - supply (circulating, total, max)
            - fully_diluted_valuation
            - market_cap
            - supply_schedule (inflation rate if available)
            - token_use (utility vs governance)
        """
        cache_key = f"tokenomics:{coin_id}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        market = self.get_market_data(coin_id)
        if not market:
            return {}

        tokenomics = {
            "coin_id": coin_id,
            "symbol": market.get("symbol"),
            "name": market.get("name"),
            # Supply metrics
            "circulating_supply": market.get("circulating_supply"),
            "total_supply": market.get("total_supply"),
            "max_supply": market.get("max_supply"),
            # Valuation
            "current_price": market.get("current_price"),
            "market_cap": market.get("market_cap"),
            "fdv": market.get("fdv"),
            "fdv_market_cap_ratio": (
                market.get("fdv") / market.get("market_cap")
                if market.get("market_cap") and market.get("fdv")
                else None
            ),
            # Supply type
            "supply_type": self._classify_supply(market),
            # Rank
            "market_cap_rank": market.get("market_cap_rank"),
        }

        self._set_cache(cache_key, tokenomics)
        return tokenomics

    def _classify_supply(self, market: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
        """Classify supply model based on supply fields."""
        max_supply = market.get("max_supply")
        total_supply = market.get("total_supply")
        circulating = market.get("circulating_supply")

        if max_supply and max_supply > 0:
            if total_supply and abs(total_supply - max_supply) / max_supply < 0.01:
                return "fixed"
            return "capped"
        if not max_supply:
            return "infinite"
        return "unknown"

    def get_token_holders(
        self,
        coin_id: str,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get top token holders (if available via CoinGecko).
        
        Note: Full on-chain holder data requires Etherscan/DeBank.
        This provides the aggregated holder stats from CoinGecko.
        """
        cache_key = f"holders:{coin_id}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/coins/{coin_id}",
                params={
                    "tickers": "false",
                    "market_data": "false",
                    "community_data": "false",
                    "developer_data": "false",
                    "sparkline": "false",
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()

            # Holder data is limited on free tier — return what's available
            result = {
                "coin_id": coin_id,
                "market_cap_rank": raw.get("market_cap_rank"),
                "circulating_supply": raw.get("market_data", {}).get("circulating_supply"),
                "community_data": raw.get("community_data", {}),
            }
            self._set_cache(cache_key, result)
            return result

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko token holders failed: {e}")
            return {}

    def get_coins_list(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
        """
        Get list of all supported coins (id, symbol, name).
        Cached aggressively — refreshes every 24h.
        """
        cache_key = "coins_list"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key) and self._cache.get(cache_key, (0, []))[0] > time.time() - 86400:
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/coins/list",
                timeout=30,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data: List[Dict[str, str]] = resp.json()
            self._set_cache(cache_key, data)
            return data

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko coins list failed: {e}")
            return []

    # ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _is_cached(self, key: str) -> bool:
        if key in self._cache:
            ts, _ = self._cache[key]
            return (time.time() - ts) < self.cache_ttl
        return False

    def _get_cached(self, key: str) -> Any:
        return self._cache[key][1]

    def _set_cache(self, key: str, val: Any) -> None:
        self._cache[key] = (time.time(), val)
        if len(self._cache) > 500:
            oldest = min(self._cache, key=lambda k: self._cache[k][0])
            del self._cache[oldest]

    def clear_cache(self) -> None:
        self._cache.clear()

{"content": """"
Tokenomics Analyzer — supply, vesting, allocation, and FDV analysis.
Combines CoinGecko, Etherscan, and DeBank data to assess token health.
"""

import os
import time
import logging
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass

import requests

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

ETHERSCAN_API_KEY = os.getenv("ETHERSCAN_API_KEY", "")
COINGECKO_BASE_URL = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3"


@dataclass
class TokenAllocation:
    """Represents a single token allocation / vesting tranche."""
    holder_type: str  # "team", "investor", "public_sale", "ecosystem", "community"
    percentage: float
    amount: Optional[float] = None
    start_date: Optional[str] = None
    end_date: Optional[str] = None
    cliff_months: int = 0
    linearly_unlocked_over: int = 0  # months


@dataclass
class TokenomicsReport:
    """Complete tokenomics snapshot."""
    token_address: str
    symbol: str
    name: str
    # Supply
    total_supply: float
    circulating_supply: float
    max_supply: Optional[float]
    # FDV
    fdv_usd: float
    market_cap_usd: float
    fdv_mc_ratio: float  # FDV / Market Cap — high ratio = high future dilution
    # Supply model
    supply_type: str  # fixed | capped | infinite
    # Price
    current_price: float
    # Allocations
    allocations: List[TokenAllocation]
    # Vesting progress
    vesting_completed_pct: float
    # Signals
    risk_signals: List[str]


class TokenomicsAnalyzer:
    """
    Analyze tokenomics for any ERC20 token.

    Combines:
      - CoinGecko: price, market data, supply
      - Etherscan: token supply, holder count
      - DeBank: top holder concentration
      - On-chain: total supply verification

    Usage:
        analyzer = TokenomicsAnalyzer()
        report = analyzer.analyze("0x6982508145454ce325ddbe97a35331eaffd0e4ee")
        print(report.fdv_mc_ratio)   # higher = more dilution risk
        print(report.risk_signals)    # list of risk flags
    """

    KNOWN_HONEYPOT_TOKENS = []  # Populated from external list or community data

    def __init__(
        self,
        etherscan_key: Optional[str] = None,
        coingecko_client: Optional[Any] = None,
        cache_ttl: int = 300,
    ):
        self.etherscan_key = etherscan_key or ETHERSCAN_API_KEY
        self.coingecko = coingecko_client
        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
        self._cache: Dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] = {}

    def analyze(self, token_address: str) -> TokenomicsReport:
        """
        Generate a full tokenomics report for a token.
        """
        cache_key = f"tokenomics_report:{token_address}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        # Resolve CoinGecko coin ID from address
        coin_id = self._resolve_coingecko_id(token_address)

        # Gather data from multiple sources in parallel-friendly manner
        cg_data = self._fetch_coingecko(coin_id) if coin_id else {}
        es_data = self._fetch_etherscan_supply(token_address)

        # Build report
        total_supply = es_data.get("total_supply") or cg_data.get("total_supply")
        circulating = cg_data.get("circulating_supply") or total_supply
        max_supply = cg_data.get("max_supply")
        fdv = cg_data.get("fdv") or 0.0
        market_cap = cg_data.get("market_cap") or 0.0
        current_price = cg_data.get("current_price") or 0.0

        # FDV / Market Cap ratio — key dilution indicator
        fdv_mc_ratio = fdv / market_cap if market_cap > 0 else 0.0

        # Vesting progress estimation (from public sale data)
        vesting_pct = self._estimate_vesting_progress(cg_data)

        # Risk signals
        risks = self._generate_risk_signals(
            token_address=token_address,
            total_supply=total_supply,
            circulating_supply=circulating,
            max_supply=max_supply,
            fdv_mc_ratio=fdv_mc_ratio,
            cg_data=cg_data,
        )

        report = TokenomicsReport(
            token_address=token_address,
            symbol=cg_data.get("symbol", "").upper(),
            name=cg_data.get("name", ""),
            total_supply=total_supply or 0.0,
            circulating_supply=circulating or 0.0,
            max_supply=max_supply,
            fdv_usd=fdv,
            market_cap_usd=market_cap,
            fdv_mc_ratio=fdv_mc_ratio,
            supply_type=self._classify_supply_type(total_supply, max_supply),
            current_price=current_price,
            allocations=[],  # Requires manual / external data
            vesting_completed_pct=vesting_pct,
            risk_signals=risks,
        )

        self._set_cache(cache_key, report)
        return report

    def get_supply_schedule(self, token_address: str) -> List[TokenAllocation]:
        """
        Estimate supply schedule based on public data.
        
        This requires external vesting data (e.g., from token distribution docs).
        Returns estimated allocations if known, empty list otherwise.
        """
        cache_key = f"supply_schedule:{token_address}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        # Attempt to fetch from Etherscan token page
        allocations = self._scrape_vesting_from_etherscan(token_address)
        self._set_cache(cache_key, allocations)
        return allocations

    def get_holder_concentration(
        self,
        token_address: str,
        top_n: int = 10,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Estimate holder concentration.
        
        Uses CoinGecko holder data where available.
        For full on-chain holder lists, use DeBank + Etherscan top holders.
        
        Returns:
            - top_holders_pct: % held by top N addresses
            - holder_count: total estimated holders
            - concentration_risk: "low" | "medium" | "high"
        """
        cache_key = f"holder_conc:{token_address}:{top_n}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        holder_count = self._fetch_holder_count(token_address)
        # Estimate concentration — on free tier, best we can do is flag risk
        result = {
            "token_address": token_address,
            "holder_count": holder_count,
            "top_holders_pct": None,  # requires premium API
            "concentration_risk": "unknown",
        }

        # Heuristic: low holder count + high FDV/MC ratio = high risk
        coin_id = self._resolve_coingecko_id(token_address)
        cg = self._fetch_coingecko(coin_id) if coin_id else {}
        fdv = cg.get("fdv", 0)
        mcap = cg.get("market_cap", 0)
        if mcap > 0 and fdv / mcap > 5 and holder_count and holder_count < 1000:
            result["concentration_risk"] = "high"
        elif holder_count and holder_count > 10000:
            result["concentration_risk"] = "low"

        self._set_cache(cache_key, result)
        return result

    def compare_tokenomics(
        self,
        token_a: str,
        token_b: str,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Side-by-side tokenomics comparison of two tokens.
        Useful for $PEPE vs $WOJAK style meme coin comparisons.
        """
        report_a = self.analyze(token_a)
        report_b = self.analyze(token_b)

        def make_score(r: TokenomicsReport) -> float:
            score = 0.0
            if r.fdv_mc_ratio < 1.5:
                score += 30
            elif r.fdv_mc_ratio < 3.0:
                score += 15
            if r.concentration_risk == "low":
                score += 25
            elif r.concentration_risk == "medium":
                score += 10
            if r.supply_type in ("fixed", "capped"):
                score += 20
            if not any("honeypot" in s.lower() for s in r.risk_signals):
                score += 15
            return score

        return {
            "token_a": {"symbol": report_a.symbol, "fdv_mc_ratio": report_a.fdv_mc_ratio, "score": make_score(report_a)},
            "token_b": {"symbol": report_b.symbol, "fdv_mc_ratio": report_b.fdv_mc_ratio, "score": make_score(report_b)},
            "winner": report_a.symbol if make_score(report_a) > make_score(report_b) else report_b.symbol,
        }

    # ─── Private: data fetching ──────────────────────────────────────────

    def _resolve_coingecko_id(self, token_address: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Try to find CoinGecko coin ID from token address via search."""
        cache_key = f"cg_id:{token_address}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        # Try searching by contract address
        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/search",
                params={"query": token_address},
                timeout=10,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                results = resp.json().get("coins", [])
                for coin in results:
                    contracts = coin.get("api_symbol", "")
                    if token_address.lower() in str(coin):
                        self._set_cache(cache_key, coin.get("id"))
                        return coin.get("id")
        except Exception:
            pass

        self._set_cache(cache_key, None)
        return None

    def _fetch_coingecko(self, coin_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        if not coin_id:
            return {}
        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                f"{COINGECKO_BASE_URL}/coins/{coin_id}",
                params={
                    "localization": "false",
                    "tickers": "false",
                    "market_data": "true",
                    "community_data": "false",
                    "developer_data": "false",
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                raw = resp.json()
                md = raw.get("market_data", {})
                return {
                    "coin_id": coin_id,
                    "symbol": raw.get("symbol", ""),
                    "name": raw.get("name", ""),
                    "current_price": md.get("current_price", {}).get("usd"),
                    "market_cap": md.get("market_cap", {}).get("usd"),
                    "fdv": md.get("fully_diluted_valuation", {}).get("usd"),
                    "circulating_supply": md.get("circulating_supply"),
                    "total_supply": md.get("total_supply"),
                    "max_supply": md.get("max_supply"),
                    "market_cap_rank": raw.get("market_cap_rank"),
                    "price_change_24h": md.get("price_change_24h"),
                    "price_change_percentage_30d": md.get("price_change_percentage_30d"),
                }
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"CoinGecko fetch failed for {coin_id}: {e}")
        return {}

    def _fetch_etherscan_supply(self, token_address: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get total supply from Etherscan token supply endpoint."""
        if not self.etherscan_key:
            return {}

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                "https://api.etherscan.io/api",
                params={
                    "module": "stats",
                    "action": "tokensupply",
                    "contractaddress": token_address,
                    "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                data = resp.json()
                if data.get("status") == "1":
                    supply_wei = int(data.get("result", "0"))
                    return {"total_supply": supply_wei / 1e18}
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Etherscan supply fetch failed: {e}")
        return {}

    def _fetch_holder_count(self, token_address: str) -> Optional[int]:
        """Get token holder count from Etherscan."""
        if not self.etherscan_key:
            return None

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                "https://api.etherscan.io/api",
                params={
                    "module": "token",
                    "action": "tokenholdercount",
                    "contractaddress": token_address,
                    "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                data = resp.json()
                if data.get("status") == "1":
                    return int(data.get("result", 0))
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Etherscan holder count failed: {e}")
        return None

    def _scrape_vesting_from_etherscan(self, token_address: str) -> List[TokenAllocation]:
        """Try to get vesting schedule from Etherscan (limited free data)."""
        # Etherscan free tier doesn't expose vesting schedules
        # This would need premium or manual research
        return []

    # ─── Private: analysis helpers ───────────────────────────────────────

    def _classify_supply_type(
        self,
        total_supply: Optional[float],
        max_supply: Optional[float],
    ) -> str:
        if max_supply and max_supply > 0:
            if total_supply and abs(total_supply - max_supply) / max_supply < 0.01:
                return "fixed"
            return "capped"
        return "infinite"

    def _estimate_vesting_progress(self, cg_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> float:
        """Rough estimate of vesting progress from circulating vs total supply."""
        total = cg_data.get("total_supply")
        circulating = cg_data.get("circulating_supply")
        if total and circulating and total > 0:
            return min(100.0, circulating / total * 100)
        return 0.0

    def _generate_risk_signals(
        self,
        token_address: str,
        total_supply: Optional[float],
        circulating_supply: Optional[float],
        max_supply: Optional[float],
        fdv_mc_ratio: float,
        cg_data: Dict[str, Any],
    ) -> List[str]:
        signals = []

        # Honeypot check
        if token_address.lower() in [a.lower() for a in self.KNOWN_HONEYPOT_TOKENS]:
            signals.append("honeypot_known")

        # No max supply = inflationary
        if not max_supply:
            signals.append("inflationary_supply_no_cap")

        # Extreme FDV/MC ratio — massive future dilution
        if fdv_mc_ratio > 10:
            signals.append("extreme_future_dilution")
        elif fdv_mc_ratio > 5:
            signals.append("high_future_dilution_risk")

        # Very low circulating supply
        if total_supply and circulating_supply and total_supply > 0:
            circ_pct = circulating_supply / total_supply
            if circ_pct < 0.1:
                signals.append("less_than_10%_circulating")
            elif circ_pct < 0.25:
                signals.append("less_than_25%_circulating")

        # Recently launched (30d price change NaN or very new)
        if cg_data.get("market_cap_rank") and cg_data.get("market_cap_rank") < 10:
            if cg_data.get("price_change_percentage_30d") is None:
                signals.append("insufficient_price_history")

        # Extremely low market cap
        mcap = cg_data.get("market_cap") or 0
        if mcap > 0 and mcap < 50_000:
            signals.append("extremely_low_market_cap")

        return signals

            del self._cache[oldest]

{"path": "/home/marco/.hermes/skills/trading/dyor-onchain-analytics/skill.md", "content": "# dyor-onchain-analytics

**Category:** trading / onchain-analytics
**Do Your Own Research — Onchain Analytics Skill**

Real-time and historical onchain analytics for crypto trading: whale tracking, wallet profiling via DeBank/Footprint, DEX trade flow, smart money detection, tokenomics analysis, and price data.

---

## Capabilities

### Whale Tracking
- Monitor large transactions (configurable threshold in USD)
- Track sudden token accumulation/distribution patterns
- Alert on whale transfers between exchanges and wallets
- Historical whale activity replay for pattern analysis
- Exchange flow detection (Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, etc.)

### DeBank Integration
- Portfolio tracking for any ENS or 0x address
- DeBank Pro API for professional data access
- Asset allocation, DeFi positions, transaction history
- Gas spending analytics
- Social simulation / identity data

### Footprint Analytics Integration
- NFT floor prices and collection analytics
- DEX liquidity data (GMX, dYdX, etc.)
- Wallet labeling and cluster analysis
- Cross-chain data (Ethereum, BSC, Polygon, Arbitrum, etc.)
- Smart money address list with P&L estimates

### DEX Data & Trade Flow (The Graph)
- Real-time trade monitoring on Uniswap v2/v3, SushiSwap, Curve
- Buy/sell pressure indicators
- DEX liquidity changes and pool creation alerts
- Sandwich attack detection heuristics
- Cross-protocol token flow aggregation

### CoinGecko Integration
- Real-time and historical price data
- Market data: market cap, volume, ATH/ATL, 24h change
- Supply metrics: circulating, total, max supply
- FDV (Fully Diluted Valuation) calculation
- Token search and trending coins
- Market chart data (prices, volumes, market caps over time)

### Tokenomics Analysis
- Supply model classification: fixed / capped / infinite
- FDV / Market Cap ratio (key dilution risk indicator)
- Vesting progress estimation (% circulating)
- Holder concentration risk scoring
- Risk signal generation:
  - `extreme_future_dilution` (FDV/MC > 10x)
  - `high_future_dilution_risk` (FDV/MC > 5x)
  - `less_than_10%_circulating`
  - `inflationary_supply_no_cap`
  - `extremely_low_market_cap`
- Side-by-side token comparison

### Smart Money Detection
- Identify known smart money wallets
- Track institutional-grade wallets
- Copy-trading signal generation
- Historical P&L of tracked wallets
- Wallet clustering (related address detection)

---

## API Keys Required

```env
DEBANK_API_KEY=***         # Free tier: 1000 calls/day  — https://open.debank.com/
COINGECKO_API_KEY=***      # Free tier: 10-50 calls/min, 10k/month — https://www.coingecko.com/en/api
FOOTPRINT_API_KEY=***      # Free tier available — https://www.footprint.network/
ETHERSCAN_API_KEY=***      # Free, multiple keys OK — https://etherscan.io/apis
ALCHEMY_API_KEY=***        # Free tier, optional — https://www.alchemy.com/
```

---

## Usage Examples

```
@skill dyor-onchain-analytics

> Track whale activity for 0x...abc (any Ethereum address)
> Show DeBank portfolio for vitalik.eth
> What are the top smart money wallets holding $PEPE?
> Monitor Uniswap v3 USDC/ETH pool for large trades
> Get token holder count and concentration for 0x...xyz
> Alert me when a whale buys >$500k of $XYZ
> Get FDV/MC ratio for $DOGE and $SHIB — which has more dilution risk?
> Compare tokenomics of 0x...abc vs 0x...xyz
> Show trending coins on CoinGecko right now
> Get holder concentration risk for 0x...token
```

---

## Architecture

```
dyor-onchain-analytics/
├── skill.md
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── whale_tracker.py      # WhaleTracker, WhaleAlert
│   ├── debank_client.py       # DeBankClient
│   ├── footprint_client.py    # FootprintClient
│   ├── dex_data.py            # DexDataProvider, DexTrade, PoolLiquidity
│   ├── coingecko_client.py    # CoinGeckoClient
│   └── tokenomics.py          # TokenomicsAnalyzer, TokenomicsReport, TokenAllocation
└── requirements.txt
```

```
OnchainAnalyticsError  ← base exception raised by all modules

WhaleTracker           # Ethereum/wallet-level tx monitoring
DeBankClient           # Portfolio & DeFi position queries
FootprintClient        # NFT, cross-chain, wallet labeling
DexDataProvider        # DEX trade flow & liquidity (The Graph)
CoinGeckoClient        # Price, market data, supply, tokenomics
TokenomicsAnalyzer     # Token supply, FDV/MC, vesting, risk signals
```

All clients return normalized Python dicts/dataclasses. Errors raise `OnchainAnalyticsError`.

---

## Pitfalls

- **Rate limits**: DeBank free tier is 1000 req/day — cache aggressively. CoinGecko free tier is 10-50 req/min.
- **Address labels**: DeBank/Footprint labels are community-sourced — verify independently.
- **Whale threshold**: Adjust per token market cap (set too low = noise, too high = missed signals).
- **DEX data lag**: The Graph subgraphs have ~15min delay on historical queries.
- **Wallet clustering**: Heuristic-based — not always accurate for layered privacy.
- **Cross-chain**: Footprint covers multiple chains but depth varies significantly.
- **Smart money**: Past performance ≠ future results — wallets can exit and reposition.
- **Tokenomics**: Vesting schedule data requires manual research; analyzer returns risk signals from on-chain data only.
- **FDV/MC ratio**: High ratio alone doesn't mean "sell" — context matters (token utility, emission schedule).
- **Holder concentration**: Free APIs don't expose full top-holder lists — use DeBank Pro for accurate concentration data.
- **CoinGecko coin ID**: Memes and new tokens may not be indexed — search by contract address may fail.

---

```
│   ├── dex_data.py
│   ├── coingecko_client.py     # ← NEW
│   └── tokenomics.py           # ← NEW
└── requirements.txt
```
